name: OVARIAN MALIGNANT GERM CELL TUMOR
definition: OMGCT = heterogeneous group of tumors sharing the primitive germ cell of the embryonic malignant germ cell tumor of ovary homologous to testicular seminoma EMBRYONAL TERATOMA = MALIGNANT TERATOMA SOLID TERATOMA enlarging teratoma of mature elements arising during / after chemotherapy for treatment composed of > 1 germ cell element (mainly dysgerminoma, teratoma, and yolk sac tumor) usually component of mixed OMGCT (immature teratoma + yolk sac tumor), extremely rare ENDODERMAL SINUS TUMOR OF OVARY

demographics.Frequency: 1-2% of all primary ovarian neoplasms; 35% of all ovarian malignant germ cell
demographics.Path: solid well encapsulated lobulated tumor ± coagulative necrosis / hemorrhage
demographics.Histo: uniform population of round cells with clear to eosinophilic cytoplasm + central large

discriminating_features: homogeneously solid (3%), predominantly solid (85%), predominantly cystic (12%)
multilobulated well-defined solid mass divided by fibrovascular enhancing septa

general: homogeneously solid (3%), predominantly solid (85%), predominantly cystic (12%)
multilobulated well-defined solid mass divided by fibrovascular enhancing septa
MR: mass of low signal intensity relative to muscle on T1WI
isointense / slightly hyperintense mass on T2WI
hypo- / isointense septa on T2WI with contrast enhancement on T1WI
wide variety of signal intensities on T2WI
solid mass + areas of cystic change ← hemorrhage / necrosis / cystic lesion with solid
abnormal large signal voids on T2WI ← vascular structures + small cystic cavities
high-signal-intensity foci in solid portions on T1WI ← hemorrhage
substantial uptake of contrast material in solid portions ← highly vascularized tumor
unilateral smoothly contoured enhancing mixed solid + cystic mass
"bright dot" sign = enhancing foci in wall / solid component of tumor (common) ← dilated
cystic areas ← epithelial-lined cysts / cysts of coexisting mature teratoma / hemorrhage /
foci of high signal intensity on T1WI ← hemorrhage
US: prominent arterial color Doppler flow within septa
predominantly solid tumor with numerous cysts of varying size + intratumoral fat
scattered small calcifications ← invariable association with mature teratoma
heterogeneous solid mass + scattered small calcifications
areas of increased echogenicity = larger fat components
well-defined adnexal mass of mixed echogenicity

clinical.Prognosis: responsible for 30% of deaths from ovarian cancer in women < 20 years
